reasoning: The market price of 9% reflects favorite-longshot bias typical in political prediction markets. Starmer was elected with a massive majority (+170 seats) in July 2024. No UK Prime Minister with such a strong electoral mandate has been ousted by their party within their first 18 months in modern history. Ousting him would require a rebellion of 20% of Labour MPs (82 MPs) and a vote of the wider membership. While there have been minor early scandals ("freebies", Sue Gray's departure), these are nowhere near the threshold to trigger a leadership crisis. The only realistic scenarios for an exit in 2025 are a sudden severe health crisis or an unprecedented, massive personal scandal. The base rate for such events within a single year is around 1-3%. Therefore, the true probability is much lower than the market's 9%, and I estimate it at 5%.
